items.scenario: You are a senior majoring in linguistics. You have been waiting for over three weeks to hear back from Professor Halverson about your independent study proposal on Indo-European phonology, but no response has come, and the registration deadline is now ten days away.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Halverson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you originally reached out about the independent study proposal.
Describe the deadline pressure you are facing for next semester's registration.
Ask whether she could meet briefly this week to discuss the proposal.
items.subject: Following up on independent study proposal before registration closes

items.scenario: You are a part-time research assistant at a campus economics lab. Your supervisor recently restructured weekly team meetings so they now happen at 7:30 in the morning, which conflicts with another required job, and several other student staff have raised similar concerns to you informally.
items.direction: Write an email to Dr. Singh. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for the recent investment in regular team coordination.
Describe the scheduling conflict the new meeting time creates for several student staff.
Suggest a slightly later morning time or a recorded alternative for affected staff.
items.subject: Scheduling concern about the new 7:30 AM lab meeting

items.scenario: You recently ordered a refurbished electric standing desk from an online office furniture retailer. After only two weeks of use, the height-adjustment motor has stopped working entirely, and the desk arrived with two of the four cable management clips missing from the package.
items.direction: Write an email to the customer support team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the height-adjustment motor failure and the missing cable management clips.
Describe what you have already tried to resolve the motor issue.
Ask what next steps the support team can offer under the refurbished warranty.
items.subject: Refurbished standing desk — motor failure and missing accessories

items.scenario: You are a regular weekend visitor at the Cedar Park community pottery studio, which recently introduced a sign-up system that requires reserving wheels through an online portal forty-eight hours in advance. Many longtime members, including several older patrons without smartphones, have struggled to use the new system.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Okafor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Acknowledge the studio's good intentions in introducing the reservation portal.
Describe specific difficulties that older members and walk-in visitors are encountering.
Suggest a small change that would keep the portal while protecting walk-in access.
items.subject: Feedback on the new wheel reservation system
